description:
32 of 143 substrate submodules are in 'standalone' state: their in-tree `.git` is a DIRECTORY (full repo, with config + objects + COMMIT_EDITMSG inline) rather than the canonical `.git` FILE containing `gitdir: ../../...../.git/modules/<submodule-path>`. They are functional individually as git repos but are decoupled from mono root's submodule machinery: parent's `.git/modules/<path>/` is empty (or missing) for all 32. The 6 submodules just renamed by mono root commit a4a1c3a45b (operators/ → collaborators/) are in this state, which is what surfaced the issue.

Full list (32):
  agents (13): allora, cameron, carrie, castor, catch, claw, cloud, common, patch, prime, quill, root, tau
  collaborators (7): amanda-champagne, jakor, johnwhitecastle, khegh, michelle-coach, natashenough, tkoh
  collectives (5): ghost-shell-org, kura-org, schema-universal, tau-app, todo3
  harness (1): gemini
  harvest (1): tempo-agent (retired)
  maintainers (3): garrytan/gstack, getyourguide, google/googleworkspace-cli
  public (2): rismay.github.io, public-readme

WHY THIS MATTERS:
  - `.gitmodules` entries point at them and parent's tree records the gitlink, but the .git/modules/ side is missing — parent submodule machinery (clone/update/init) doesn't see them.
  - A naive `git submodule update --init` could attempt to re-clone these, which would attempt to clobber the in-tree .git data — DATA LOSS RISK if uncommitted work is present.
  - Tools that walk `.git/modules/` for cached submodule state silently skip these 32.
  - The discrepancy between the parent's pointer record and the in-tree .git's actual state can cause subtle drift when running tools that rely on the canonical layout.

THE CANONICAL 2-STEP FIX — NOT deinit+add:
  `git submodule absorbgitdirs <path>` is the operator-canonical, non-destructive fix.
  Per `man git-submodule`: 'If a git directory of a submodule is inside the submodule, move the git directory of the submodule into its superproject's $GIT_DIR/modules path and then connect the git directory and its working directory by setting the core.worktree and adding a .git file pointing to the git directory embedded in the superprojects git directory.'
  This is exactly the 2-step process: (1) relocate the standalone .git into .git/modules/<path>/, (2) replace in-tree .git directory with a .git file pointer. Preserves all data — no re-clone.

  DO NOT use `git submodule deinit <path>` + `git submodule add <url> <path>` — that path would WIPE the standalone .git data including any unpushed work.

SAFETY CHECKS BEFORE RUNNING ABSORBGITDIRS:
  1. For each of the 32 submodules, run `git status --short` from within the submodule. ANY dirty state needs to be either committed or stashed FIRST (absorbgitdirs preserves worktree state but verifying clean is safer).
  2. Verify the submodule's HEAD matches `git submodule status` (no drift between in-tree HEAD and parent's pointer).
  3. Check `.git/modules/<path>/` does NOT already exist (would indicate prior partial absorb attempt — needs investigation, not absorb).
  4. Run on ONE submodule first; verify the .git directory became a .git file pointing at `.git/modules/<path>/`; verify `git submodule status` is still clean afterwards.
  5. Then batch the remaining 31 by category (e.g., agents first, then collaborators, then collectives).

SUGGESTED PER-CATEGORY COMMITS (cascade pattern):
  - One mono root commit per category: `chore(submodules): absorbgitdirs for <N> <category> submodules`
  - Each commit captures (a) the .gitmodules update if any (absorbgitdirs may modify it), (b) the .git/modules/ moves which are NOT in mono root's tree (live in .git/ which is gitignored), (c) verification via `git submodule status` no-anomalies.
  - Since absorbgitdirs touches `.git/modules/` (which is gitignored) and possibly `.gitmodules` (tracked), the COMMIT often has zero or one-line diff at mono root. The bulk of the work is .git/-side relocation, not tree changes.

ROUTE:
  Open mono root commit a4a1c3a45b that triggered the discovery: jakor/johnwhitecastle/tkoh/khegh/amanda-champagne/natashenough rename. Either re-run absorbgitdirs for these 6 first (closes the rename properly) or batch all 32. Operator's call.

RELATED:
  - substrate-wide-cascade-pattern.axiom.su.json: the absorbgitdirs sweep itself is a substrate-wide cascade — proceed via per-category commits + verification gate.
  - winddown-incomplete-until-committed.axiom.su.json: don't leave the substrate in mid-absorb state; each category commit must be a clean stopping point.
  - no-git-add-dash-uppercase-a.axiom.su.json: never bulk-stage absorbgitdirs output — it touches gitignored .git/modules/.
resolution: RESOLVED 2026-06-01: 32 standalone submodules absorbed to canonical state via `git submodule absorbgitdirs` per the 2-step doctrine. Canary executed on tempo-agent (retired); batch on remaining 31 via sequential loop. Post-cascade verification: 139 canonical (.git file → .git/modules/<section-name>/), 0 standalone, 4 missing-worktree (separate fix pattern documented). Three typed substrate-canonical records authored per /capture protocol: axiom (submodule-moves-are-2-step-process), workflow (substrate-submodule-integrity-repair v0.1.0), role (substrate-integrity-steward). Mono root commit 1fb8e2a9ec captures the typed records + Techo chronicle. NO .gitmodules diffs (absorbgitdirs only touches gitignored .git/modules/).
title: Repair 32 standalone submodules via `git submodule absorbgitdirs` (NOT deinit+add)
